details.summary: Glama.ai requires manual submission via web browser with GitHub OAuth authentication. There is no public REST API for programmatic submission. The service is a client-rendered Next.js application. All API endpoints tested (/api/servers, /api/github/submit) return HTTP 404. The /api/mcp/servers endpoint exists but returns an HTML page (Next.js app), not a REST API. Submission flow requires: (1) Log in at glama.ai via GitHub OAuth with write access to Conway-Research/automaton, (2) Navigate to MCP servers page, (3) Submit the repository URL for automated scanning and verification.

endpoint_checks.https://glama.ai/api/servers: HTTP 404 - No API endpoint for submission
endpoint_checks.https://glama.ai/api/mcp/servers: HTTP 200 - Client-rendered HTML page (not a REST API)
endpoint_checks.https://glama.ai/api/github/submit: HTTP 404 - No API for GitHub submission

submission_process.auth_required: GitHub account with write/admin access to Conway-Research/automaton
steps: 1. Open https://glama.ai and sign up via GitHub OAuth
5. Glama verifies write access via GitHub OAuth
6. Glama clones the repo, builds Docker image, runs MCP introspection and behavioral analysis

credentials_available.env_vars: No GITHUB_TOKEN or Glama-specific credentials found

details.research_notes: Glama scans repositories automatically upon submission. It builds the Docker image, runs the MCP server in an isolated Firecracker microVM, performs protocol introspection (tools/list, resources/list, prompts/list), runs behavioral analysis, and computes a Tool Definition Quality Score (TDQS). The listing is preserved even if build fails but hidden from search results.

recommendation: Manual submission is required. A human with GitHub write access to Conway-Research/automaton must: (1) Log into glama.ai via GitHub OAuth, (2) Navigate to https://glama.ai/mcp/servers, (3) Click 'Submit Server', (4) Enter the repository URL. The `glama_manual.md` file contains detailed step-by-step instructions, prerequisite checklist, metadata reference, post-submission checklist, and troubleshooting guide. Ensure the Dockerfile builds successfully (listens on port 8080) before submitting.
